title: Cipher Digital secures $200M credit line to scale AI and HPC footprint
text: Nasdaq-listed Cipher Digital has secured a $200 million revolving credit facility backed by a syndicate of global financial institutions to support its strategic shift from Bitcoin mining toward artificial intelligence and high-performance computing data center operations. The facility provides committed capacity with an accordion option of up to $50 million, matures in March 2030, and carries pricing at SOFR plus 1.25%-1.75% with step-downs based on debt-to-market-cap ratios. According to the company, proceeds will enhance liquidity, support working capital, and fund growth initiatives, including construction of multiple AI and HPC data center campuses. In Q1 2026, Cipher reported $35 million in revenue alongside a $48 million adjusted EBITDA loss, reflecting heavy upfront investments in repositioning. The company simultaneously announced execution of a 15-year lease agreement for its third large AI data center campus with an investment-grade hyperscale tenant. CFO Greg Mumford described the facility as a major step in the evolution of the company's capital structure, while CEO Tyler Page framed the third campus lease as validation that Cipher's position as a trusted partner remains strong in developing HPC infrastructure for major companies.
